summary: MECT EW is a single-page web app deployed on the Internet Computer (Motoko canister backend + React frontend) for publishing categorized financial market analysis videos and collecting enquiries (contact/consultancy/mentoring). The site supports Internet Identity authentication, role-based admin controls, video upload (screen recording + manual upload), public video browsing by category, admin video management (search/filter, preview, download, delete, edit title, thumbnail management), and admin message management (view/filter, reply, delete). It also includes an admin-facing custom domain/DNS verification workflow with backend HTTP outcalls and frontend guidance UI.
architectural_notes: ICP canister backend written in Motoko with modular mixins: blob-storage mixin (caffeine storage lifecycle/GC helpers) and authorization mixin (access control API).

Role-based access control implemented via an in-canister Map from Principal -> role (#admin/#user/#guest). Initialization is performed from the frontend by calling `_initializeAccessControlWithSecret`, using a secret token compared against an environment variable (`CAFFEINE_ADMIN_TOKEN`).
Backend state uses `mo:core/Map` collections for videos, thumbnails, messages, pending videos, and user profiles; upgrade migrations handled via `with migration = Migration.run`.
Frontend is a React SPA using TanStack Router for routing and TanStack React Query for data fetching/mutations and cache invalidation; UI is Tailwind + shadcn components + next-themes dark mode.

Frontend constructs an authenticated/anonymous canister actor via `createActorWithConfig` depending on Internet Identity state, and uses URL-hash secret extraction (`caffeineAdminToken`) persisted in sessionStorage.
Large file transfers use `ExternalBlob` (ICP SDK / backend binding) to upload bytes with progress callbacks, enabling upload progress, speed, and ETA tracking in the UI.
Admin UX is largely enforced at the backend (traps on unauthorized access) and mirrored in the frontend by gating admin tools/pages based on `isCallerAdmin`.
Custom domain verification includes backend HTTP outcalls (`ic:aaaaa-aa` http_request) with a transform function to strip headers.
Domain management UX extended to include an admin-accessible 'Force verification' action to re-trigger backend DNS verification checks when automatic verification stalls.
Video administration should differentiate public vs admin capabilities: public streaming only; admin-only download and management actions (rename, thumbnail upload).
known_issues: Anonymous visitors likely trigger an error in `useGetCallerUserProfile()`: the Layout always runs the query once an anonymous actor exists, but backend `getCallerUserProfile` traps unless caller has #user permission (guests/anonymous will fail). The query has `retry: false` but will still be in an error state for unauthenticated users unless additionally gated.

Frontend has nested `QueryClientProvider`s (one in `main.tsx`, another in `App.tsx`) with different clients; this is redundant and can cause confusion or inconsistent cache usage if any hooks run outside the inner provider.

`uploadThumbnail` backend method ignores the provided image format (always stores `#jpg`) and does not update the referenced video’s `thumbnailUrl`/`thumbnail` fields; therefore “auto-generated/manual URL thumbnails” uploaded via this method may not actually appear in galleries that rely on `getVideoThumbnailBlob` and/or `video.thumbnailUrl` linkage.

`ScreenRecorder.tsx` uses `declare const ExternalBlob: any;` rather than importing the generated `ExternalBlob` binding (unlike `ManualVideoUpload.tsx`), which can break if the global is not available in the build/runtime.

Domain management is partly placeholder/prototype: `DomainManagement.tsx` uses `canisterId = 'your-canister-id'` and client-side DNS checks via Google DNS; backend `retryVerification()` uses a hardcoded domain (`mectelliottwave.com`) and does not parse/validate the outcall response to actually confirm DNS ownership/verification.
Blob storage cashier principal env var name appears misspelled (`CAFFFEINE_STORAGE_CASHIER_PRINCIPAL` with triple 'F'); if not matched in deployment env it will trap.

Some components are placeholders/empty in the provided tree (e.g., `VideoManagement.tsx` explicitly says video management not available; several section components were provided empty), which may indicate incomplete or duplicated UI implementations.
`useDownloadVideo` always saves as `video/mp4` and `.mp4` filename even though recordings are typically `webm`; downloaded file type may not match source encoding.
Users reported intermittent admin recognition after Internet Identity login ('not authorized' after successful II), indicating unstable role/session recognition or admin bootstrap token flow issues.
Manual video upload previously failed with 'ExternalBlob is not defined', indicating a missing/incorrect import/binding in the manual upload path (regression risk).

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication context
existing_features.description: Provides an `InternetIdentityProvider` and `useInternetIdentity` hook to initialize an AuthClient, load persisted sessions, login via Internet Identity with long-lived delegation (30 days), and logout/clear identity state.

existing_features.title: Canister actor creation and identity-aware refetching
existing_features.description: Creates an anonymous or authenticated backend actor depending on identity; authenticated actors attach the identity to the agent and call `_initializeAccessControlWithSecret` with a secret from the URL hash/session. On actor change, invalidates and refetches dependent React Query queries.

existing_features.title: Role-based access control with admin bootstrap secret
existing_features.description: Motoko access control assigns roles (#admin/#user/#guest). The first non-anonymous principal to initialize with the correct secret token becomes admin; others become users. Includes APIs to get caller role, check admin status, and assign roles (admin-only).

existing_features.title: User profile storage and first-time profile setup modal
existing_features.description: Backend stores per-principal user profiles. Frontend queries caller profile and shows a blocking modal to collect and save a name for authenticated users who have no profile yet.

existing_features.title: Public pages and SPA navigation
existing_features.description: Implements routes for Home, About, Analysis, Consultancy, Mentoring, Contact, and Admin pages with a shared layout including header and footer.

existing_features.title: Contact/consultancy/mentoring forms and message persistence
existing_features.description: Frontend contact form submits name/email/message with a form type. Backend stores messages with timestamp, status (#pending/#replied), optional reply text, and form type.

existing_features.title: Admin message management (global and per-form)
existing_features.description: Admin-only message listing from backend with filtering (form type/status), reply workflow (updates status to replied), and deletion with confirmation dialogs. Includes per-form management panels embedded on relevant pages.

existing_features.title: Screen recording capture tool (admin-only UI)
existing_features.description: Browser-based screen recorder with Full HD/60fps settings, optional microphone and system audio, recording duration display, local download of recorded blob, and upload to backend with progress, speed, ETA, and automatic retry on network errors.

existing_features.title: Manual video upload (admin-only UI)
existing_features.description: Manual upload of external video files (MP4/MOV/AVI/WebM) up to 2GB with validation, preview, category selection, file type selection, progress/speed/ETA metrics, and retry handling. Uses `ExternalBlob` byte upload with progress callbacks.

existing_features.title: Video metadata storage and categorization
existing_features.description: Backend stores videos with id/title/timestamp/filesize/blob/category/source type (recorded/uploaded) and optional thumbnail fields. Supports public queries for all videos and by category.

existing_features.title: Public video gallery with on-demand streaming and thumbnail caching
existing_features.description: Displays public videos in a responsive grid per category, opens a dialog to stream/play video by fetching the blob on demand, and caches thumbnail direct URLs per video when available.

existing_features.title: Admin video management panel
existing_features.description: Admin-only management UI to list full video metadata, search and filter by category, preview videos, download, delete, and inline edit video titles. Uses React Query mutations with cache invalidation.

existing_features.title: Thumbnail management (custom upload, auto generation trigger, revert)
existing_features.description: Supports uploading custom thumbnail blobs (JPG/PNG/WebP, <=5MB) and attaching them to videos; frontend also provides an auto-thumbnail generation flow by grabbing a frame from the video and calling thumbnail upload APIs; includes ability to revert to auto/default thumbnail state.

existing_features.title: Admin-only secure blob download endpoint and client download flow
existing_features.description: Backend exposes an admin-gated `downloadBlob` (and video blob download) method. Frontend retrieves bytes and triggers a browser download for admin-managed videos.

existing_features.title: Bulk/pending video operations on backend
existing_features.description: Backend includes APIs to move videos across categories, bulk delete, bulk move, and publish pending videos to the main catalog, plus queries to list pending videos (admin-only).

existing_features.title: Custom domain verification tooling (admin)
existing_features.description: Frontend provides DNS setup instructions (GoDaddy) and verification actions. Backend provides `getCustomDomainStatus` (admin-only) and `retryVerification` which performs an HTTP outcall to a DNS resolver and updates last-attempt/status fields.

existing_features.title: Blob-storage maintenance mixin for Caffeine storage gateway
existing_features.description: Includes canister methods for storage gateway principal authorization updates, listing dead blobs for deletion, confirming pruning, forcing GC, and cashier cycle refill/top-up behavior.

existing_features.title: Camera utility hook
existing_features.description: Provides a reusable `useCamera` hook for starting/stopping camera streams, switching cameras, capturing photos to files, and handling permission/support errors (even if not currently wired into main UI).

existing_features.title: Update the Analysis admin upload UIs so the video title field is optional (not required) for both screen recordings and manual uploads.
Update the Analysis admin upload UIs so a video can be published with an empty/whitespace title, without any client-side validation blocking the upload, for both screen recordings and manual uploads.

Remove or adjust any auto-fill behavior that prevents users from intentionally leaving the title blank during manual uploads (e.g., auto-populating the title from the filename should not occur if the user wants an empty title).
Ensure user-facing helper/label text for the title field is in English and clearly indicates that the title is optional and that an automatic title will be generated if left blank.

After a screen recording is completed in the ScreenRecorder component, display an in-app video preview player so the admin can review the recorded video directly within the app before deciding to upload or download it. The preview should use the recorded Blob/MediaStream data already available in memory (no upload required to preview). The existing download button must remain visible alongside the new preview player.

In the admin Video Management Panel (`VideoManagementPanel.tsx`), update the inline title-editing UI so the title field is optional. Remove any client-side validation that prevents saving an empty or whitespace title. The helper text next to the title input must indicate in English that the title is optional and that a default title will be generated automatically if left blank.

In the frontend upload forms (ScreenRecorder and ManualVideoUpload) and the VideoManagementPanel inline title editor, remove any logic that substitutes a generated default title (e.g., 'Title 1', 'Untitled', or any auto-increment label) when the user leaves the title field blank. An intentionally blank title must be sent to the backend as-is.

In the Analysis page's video management area, for each video that has view data, display a geographic breakdown panel showing how many views originated from each country. This panel should be visible only to admin users. It should display alongside or below the existing total view count, listing each country and its corresponding view count, sorted by count descending.

In the admin Video Management Panel (VideoManagementPanel.tsx), display geographic view breakdown data for videos that already have recorded views — not only for views captured after the feature was introduced. The country breakdown panel must show all view records currently stored in `videoViewRecords`, regardless of when they were recorded. If existing view records have no country data (e.g., country is empty or unknown), display them as 'Unknown' in the breakdown list.

new_feature_requests.summary: Enable manual upload of external videos for Analysis section (in addition to in-app screen recording), with reliable format/size support
Add admin-only ability to edit uploaded video titles (rename videos) in Analysis management
Add per-video thumbnail support like YouTube: auto-generate thumbnail and allow manual thumbnail image upload/replacement
Hide video file size (MB) from public visitors; keep technical details visible to admin only
Add admin-only download controls for Analysis videos (public can only stream/view in-app)
Allow admin to download the recorded video immediately after recording (pre-upload) to support later manual upload
Add/restore admin panel tooling to force custom-domain verification (manual re-check) when automatic verification stalls
Make video title optional (not required) when uploading videos in the Analysis section
Ensure the backend accepts uploads with an empty/whitespace title by generating a default non-empty title server-side for both recorded uploads and manual uploads.
After finishing a screen recording, allow the admin to preview/review the recorded video directly within the app (in addition to the existing download option)
Allow admin to clear/make optional the title of already-uploaded Analysis videos (edit existing video titles to be blank/empty, not just during upload)

Update the backend `updateVideoTitle` (or equivalent) function so it accepts an empty or whitespace-only string as a valid title for an already-uploaded video. If the provided title is empty or whitespace, the backend should store a server-generated default title (e.g., based on upload date/time) rather than rejecting the request or trapping.

In the backend `updateVideoTitle` function (and any related upload handlers), when the provided title is empty or whitespace, store an empty string as the title rather than substituting a generated default (e.g., 'Title 1'). The backend must not auto-populate a fallback title if the caller intentionally submits a blank value.
For Analysis videos, show geographic/country breakdown of views (where in the world each view comes from), in addition to the total view count already displayed

Extend the backend video view tracking to capture and store the geographic origin (country) of each view. When a view is recorded, attempt to resolve the caller's approximate country from the request context or IP-based metadata available on the Internet Computer. Store per-video view records as a list of entries containing at minimum a country code/name and a timestamp.
Backfill/show geographic location data for existing (already recorded) video views, not just new views going forward

In the backend (main.mo), when returning per-video view records via the geographic view query function, do not filter out records that have an empty or missing country field. Ensure that all stored view records — including those recorded before country capture was implemented — are returned to the caller so the frontend can display them (grouping unknowns as 'Unknown').
